name: org.machanism.machai:project-layout
description:
Project Layout is a Java utility library for detecting, describing, and working with conventional on-disk project directory layouts (sources, tests, docs, resources) in a consistent way. It is intended for build tooling, repository scanners, and plugins that need reliable discovery of well-known folders across heterogeneous repositories.

Core abstraction: org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout models a project rooted at a filesystem directory and exposes root-relative paths for production sources, test sources, and documentation roots, and optionally nested modules. It also provides filesystem traversal helpers (findFiles/findDirectories) that recursively scan a project while excluding common build/tooling directories such as node_modules, .git, target, build, IDE metadata, and virtualenv caches.

Layout detection/orchestration: org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectLayoutManager.detectProjectLayout(java.io.File) selects an appropriate ProjectLayout implementation by inspecting standard build metadata files: pom.xml (Maven), build.gradle (Gradle), package.json (JavaScript/TypeScript), and pyproject.toml (Python). The returned layout is configured with the given projectDir.

Provided layout implementations:
- MavenProjectLayout: reads pom.xml via PomReader and exposes Maven-derived modules when packaging is pom; computes sources/tests using Maven build defaults and configured build directories and resources.
- GragleProjectLayout: uses the Gradle Tooling API to load the GradleProject model, enumerates child module names, and returns conventional roots (src/main, src/test, src/site).
- JScriptProjectLayout: parses package.json "workspaces" and resolves module directories by matching workspace glob patterns against directories containing package.json; returns module paths relative to the project root.
- PythonProjectLayout: detects Python projects primarily via pyproject.toml metadata and related heuristics; currently returns empty lists for sources/tests/docs.
- DefaultProjectLayout: minimal filesystem fallback that treats immediate subdirectories as potential modules (excluding well-known tooling/build directories) and returns empty lists for sources/tests/docs.

Notable runtime dependencies include Apache Maven Model APIs for POM parsing, Gradle Tooling API for Gradle model access, Jackson for JSON parsing, tomlj for TOML parsing, commons-lang3/commons-io utilities, and slf4j for logging.

usageContext: Tools that need to locate sources/tests/docs across Maven/Gradle/Node/Python projects

constructors.description: Creates a Maven-aware project layout instance. Configure it by calling projectDir(java.io.File) before calling accessors such as getSources(), getTests(), getDocuments(), or getModules().

examples.description: Create a MavenProjectLayout for a repository root and read Maven-derived source roots.

constructors.description: Detects and instantiates the appropriate ProjectLayout (Maven/Gradle/JS/Python/Default) based on files present in the given directory, and sets the projectDir on the returned layout.

examples.description: Detect the layout type from a project directory and enumerate modules when supported.

features.name: detectProjectLayout
features.description: Detects a project layout by inspecting build metadata files (pom.xml, build.gradle, package.json, pyproject.toml) and returns a configured ProjectLayout implementation (including a filesystem-based default).

examples.description: Detect a layout and obtain a short type name derived from the implementing class.

features.name: getRelativePath
features.signature: java.lang.String org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout.getRelativePath(java.io.File dir, java.io.File file, boolean addSingleDot)
features.description: Computes a normalized root-relative path from a project directory to a target file. Can optionally prefix the result with "./". Returns "." when the target is the base directory; returns null when the computed result indicates the target is not within the project directory (as per current implementation behavior).

examples.description: Compute a root-relative path for a file under the project root.

features.name: findDirectories
features.description: Recursively enumerates directories under a project root while excluding common build/tooling directories (e.g., node_modules, .git, target, build). Useful for lightweight repository scanning and workspace module resolution.

examples.snippet:
java.io.File root = new java.io.File("/repo");
java.util.List<java.io.File> dirs = org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout.findDirectories(root);
examples.description: Traverse project directories while skipping typical noisy folders.

features.name: getModules (Maven)
features.description: For Maven projects with packaging 'pom', returns modules declared in pom.xml; otherwise returns null (indicating no module enumeration for non-aggregator projects).

examples.description: Read declared Maven modules from an aggregator pom.xml.

features.name: getModules (Gradle)
features.description: Uses the Gradle Tooling API GradleProject model to enumerate child projects for multi-project builds; returns an empty list if the model cannot be loaded or has no children.

examples.description: List Gradle child modules using the tooling model.

features.name: getModules (JavaScript workspaces)
features.description: Parses package.json workspaces and resolves module directories by matching workspace glob patterns against directories containing package.json, returning root-relative module paths.

examples.description: Resolve Yarn/npm/pnpm workspace modules from package.json.

features.name: scanFolder
features.description: Scans a project directory, detects its layout, and processes either the root folder or recursively processes each discovered module (if the layout returns a non-null module list).

examples.description: Scan a repository, detect the layout, and process sources/tests/docs for the root project or for each module recursively.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout
studs.description: Base abstraction for describing a project's conventional on-disk layout. Implementations return root-relative paths for sources/tests/docs, optionally enumerate modules, and provide helper methods for computing relative paths and scanning directories while excluding common build/tooling folders.

examples.description: Use the ProjectLayout abstraction to obtain conventional roots regardless of the underlying build system.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor
studs.description: Abstract processor that scans a project folder, detects its layout, optionally recurses into modules, and delegates per-folder handling to processFolder(ProjectLayout). Intended to be extended by tools that need to walk repositories and act on sources/tests/docs.

examples.description: Extend ProjectProcessor to implement custom per-folder logic.

examples.title: Scan a repository and process modules recursively
examples.description: Use ProjectProcessor to detect the layout, recurse into modules when reported, and handle each project folder via processFolder(ProjectLayout).
